text:
Market today: The mood is cautious. Geopolitical tensions from the intensifying Iran crisis are driving energy prices up, which increases the likelihood of a Fed rate hike. This is a headwind for growth-oriented tech and chip names, your primary holdings.

Your holdings: SOXL is down -36.78% and SPAL is down -55.29%. Both are leveraged ETFs, making these drawdowns especially painful on margin. QQQ and QQQI are also flagged for trimming.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the Iran crisis developments and any further signals from the Fed regarding interest rates. Also, monitor your leveraged positions closely.

Bottom line: With significant leverage in your book and a challenging macro environment, risk management is paramount.
plays: Adjust SOXL's default stop from 200 to a realistic level to protect capital, given its -36.78% loss and leveraged nature.
Review SPAL, a new, highly volatile leveraged ETF down -55.29%; adjust its default stop from 21.38 to limit further downside.
Consider trimming QQQ and QQQI to manage overall tech exposure amidst rising rate hike fears.
